pattern: Comparing the array to its sorted version, the leftmost and rightmost positions that differ bound the shortest window that needs sorting.
hints: If the whole array were already sorted, no window is needed, so the answer is zero.
Comparing each element to where it would sit when sorted tells you which positions are out of place.
Sort a copy; find the first and last indices where the original differs from the sorted copy. The length is last - first + 1, or 0 if they never differ.

edge_inputs.description: only the two ends are swapped
one element far out of place near the start
sorted except last element too small

stress.description: random array, occasionally fully sorted, to test the zero case and general windows
stress.note: includes already-sorted inputs to exercise the zero answer
